page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.e-c4m1.large is an entry-level virtual machine instance belonging to the ecs.e server family. It is built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The instance features 2 virtual CPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with a single thread per core. It is configured with 0.5 GB of system memory, resulting in a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 0.25 GB per core. The CPU allocation is shared, meaning physical processor resources are multiplexed among multiple virtual machines.

This instance does not include any bundled local storage, requiring users to attach external block storage for operating system and application data. It does not feature a dedicated GPU, nor does it provide a baseline network bandwidth, which is rated at 0 Gbps. The lack of advanced CPU instruction sets, such as Advanced Vector Extensions or Cryptography acceleration flags, limits its utility for complex mathematical computations or cryptographic operations. These hardware constraints represent a significant tradeoff, prioritizing resource density and cost efficiency over raw performance.

Given its shared CPU allocation and minimal memory footprint, the ecs.e-c4m1.large is designed for lightweight, non-critical workloads. It is qualitatively positioned as a highly cost-efficient option for basic utility tasks, simple scripting, lightweight microservices, or development and testing environments that do not require sustained CPU performance or significant memory capacity. It is not suitable for database hosting, memory-intensive applications, or high-throughput web serving.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.e-c4m1.large is an entry-level virtual machine from the ecs.e family, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.5 GHz, offering 2 vCPUs and 0.5 GB of system memory. This instance features a shared CPU allocation model, which shares physical processor resources with other tenants. It does not include local storage, dedicated GPUs, or baseline network bandwidth. Due to its minimal memory capacity of 0.25 GB per core and the absence of advanced CPU instruction sets like AVX, this server is designed for low-demand, non-critical applications. It offers a highly cost-efficient profile for basic utility tasks, lightweight scripting, and development environments where resource demands are minimal and performance requirements are flexible.
